creator: lapo@lapo.it
text:
Stanislav Sedov wrote:
> Yes, lablgtk2 won't build without Tk.

Mhh, in that case it would be problably better to depend explicitly on
ocaml and not accept "ocaml-notk" (which I had installed), but OTOH I
see you're using USE_OCAML=yes and that bsd.ocaml.mk doesn't seem to
have a way to specify Tk is needed as a dependency.

In any case that doesn't seem to be the culprit as I've now re-compiled
Ocaml with X11 and TK options and I still have the same error.

Full log here: http://lapo.it/~lapo/lablgtk-2.14.0.log

PS: I also tried disabling (and even deinstalling) ccache, so that's
definitely not the issue.
